summary: Tested GPT-5.2 AI Integration and WebSocket Real-time Notifications in Enterprise Self-Healing System. All 16 backend tests passed (100%). All 19 frontend tests passed (100%). GPT-5.2 AI fix generation is working - fix descriptions contain 'GPT-5.2' reference. WebSocket events are properly configured for all self-healing lifecycle events.

issues: Minor HTML nesting warning: Badge (div) inside p tag causing React hydration warning. This is cosmetic and doesn't affect functionality.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/health - Returns 200 with status 'healthy'
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/dashboard - Returns dashboard data with auto_heal_enabled
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/handle-error with auto_fix=true triggers AI fix generation
Backend: AI-generated fixes include 'GPT-5.2' in fix_description
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/handle-error without auto_fix returns manual_required status
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/handle-error returns GPT-5.2 reference when fix generated
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/health - Returns service health (Backend API, Frontend, MongoDB all healthy)
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/critical-modules - Returns 12 protected modules
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/errors - Returns errors list
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/patterns - Returns error patterns
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/rollback-stats - Returns rollback statistics
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/learning-stats - Returns learning engine statistics
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/sandbox-results - Returns sandbox test results
Backend: WebSocket manager has all self-healing event types configured
Backend: Errors count tracking works correctly
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/toggle-auto-heal - Enable/disable works correctly
Frontend: AutoHeal AI Dashboard page loads correctly
Frontend: Enterprise tab shows 'Enterprise Self-Healing System' header
Frontend: System Status card shows 'HEALTHY' badge
Frontend: Errors Detected stat shows count (9)
Frontend: Auto-Fix Rate stat shows percentage (0.0%)
Frontend: Protected Modules stat shows count (12)
Frontend: Service Health shows Backend API (healthy, 22.24ms response)
Frontend: Service Health shows Frontend (healthy, 22.84ms response)
Frontend: Service Health shows MongoDB (healthy, 0.00ms response)
Frontend: System Metrics shows CPU Usage (12.8%)
Frontend: System Metrics shows Memory Usage (61.3%)
Frontend: System Metrics shows Disk Usage (57.0%)
Frontend: Learning Engine section present (7 total patterns, 35% avg confidence)
Frontend: Rollback Manager section present (3 active triggers)
Frontend: Critical Module Protection section shows protected modules
Frontend: Recent Operations section shows healing operations
Frontend: Refresh button present and working
Frontend: 'Disable Auto-Heal' button present (auto-heal currently enabled)
Frontend: Live WebSocket indicator showing real-time updates enabled
critical_code_review_comments: Good: GPT-5.2 AI integration working correctly via emergentintegrations library
Good: Fix descriptions properly include 'GPT-5.2' reference (e.g., 'AI-generated fix for KeyError (GPT-5.2)')

Good: WebSocket manager has all 9 self-healing event types properly defined (self_heal_error_detected, self_heal_fix_generating, self_heal_fix_testing, self_heal_deploying, self_heal_completed, self_heal_failed, self_heal_rollback, self_heal_blocked, self_heal_health_update)
Good: Frontend properly handles all WebSocket event types in handleWebSocketMessage function
Good: Real-time notifications working with toast messages for each self-healing event
Good: Enterprise tab updates in real-time when events are received
Minor: Badge component inside p tag causes HTML nesting warning at AutoHealDashboard.js line 523 - consider changing p to div
seed_data_creation: Error detection engine automatically stores errors during self-healing pipeline execution
context_for_next_testing_agent: GPT-5.2 AI integration and WebSocket notifications fully tested and working. Key findings: 1) POST /api/self-healing/handle-error with auto_fix=true triggers GPT-5.2 AI for fix generation. 2) Fix descriptions contain 'GPT-5.2' reference (e.g., 'AI-generated fix for TypeError (GPT-5.2)'). 3) All WebSocket events properly configured in websocket_manager.py. 4) Frontend properly handles WebSocket events in AutoHealDashboard.js. 5) Enterprise tab shows real-time updates. Note: WebSocket connection shows 'Live' badge when connected. Auto-heal can be toggled via 'Enable/Disable Auto-Heal' button.

gpt52_ai_integration.description: GPT-5.2 AI generates fixes via emergentintegrations library with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY

websocket_events.description: All 9 self-healing WebSocket event types configured and handled

enterprise_dashboard_realtime.description: Enterprise tab updates in real-time via WebSocket events
